purpose: The annotation-function Interval-return arm (the board's named-unobserved 'returning an Interval instead of a 2-tuple', slice-2 review L3): first_annotation_interval registers cleanly but returns the first qualifying atom's bound OBJECT, and reason() dies at the annotate seam with TypeError('bad argument type for built-in operation') — the pinned objmode Tuple((float64, float64)) coercion rejecting a non-sequence (interpretation.py:1918). The raise is the reason step's banked outcome; get_time after it compares the state the raise left behind.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_annotation_function (Interval-return arm, named unobserved until session 29); interpretation.py:1914-1931 at the pin; reference fn harness/reference_fns.py first_annotation_interval; screened live 2026-07-12 (screen_pin_arms.py ann-interval, 2 fresh pin processes, message byte-stable)
